name: Bird's Nests with Cheese
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp Mint, fresh, chopped (or 1/2 tsp Mint, dried)
3/4 cup Cheese, Kasseri, crumbled OR 3/4 cup Cheese, Feta, crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine eggs, Parmesan, ricotta, mint, and pepper in a mixing bowl. Set aside.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F.
3. Cut phyllo pastry sheets in half crosswise and cover with waxed paper and a lightly dampened kitchen towel.
4. Place two half sheets of phyllo on a work surface, horizontally, trim off and discard a 1-inch strip from one short edge.
5. Pull the top sheet toward you so it overlaps the bottom sheet by about 1 1/2 inches. Dot exposed surfaces with clarified butter.
6. Place a dowel or no. 11 knitting needle about 1 1/2 inches from the long edge farthest from you. Fold the edge over the dowel.
7. Spread about 1 teaspoon of filling along the edge of the folded flap. Fold the dowel over the filling, then carefully roll up the phyllo toward you, leaving a 1 1/2 inch strip at the bottom edge.
8. Holding the edges of the pastry at the ends of the dowel, gently press them towards the center, forming pleats as if on a curtain rod. (Do not worry if some filling oozes out.)
9. Firmly holding the ends of the pastry to maintain pleats, carefully pull out the dowel. Bring the rolled edges of the pastry around, forming a circle; the unrolled border will form the bottom of each bird's nest.
10. Transfer the bird's nest to an ungreased baking sheet. Brush all exposed surfaces of the pastry generously with clarified butter.
11. Bake until golden, about 15 minutes.
12. Remove the baking sheet from the oven and place about 1 1/2 tablespoons of Kasseri (or Feta) in the center of each nest. Return to the oven until the cheese is bubbly, about 3 to 4 minutes.
13. Place an olive in the center of each nest.
14. Serve hot.
